data.title: UPDATE test + SKILL.md: Verify sprint SKILL.md references sprint-pause-state.sh and add pause-resume section
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
Create tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh with structural boundary tests BEFORE updating sprint SKILL.md. Tests must fail (RED) because sprint SKILL.md does not yet reference sprint-pause-state.sh. After SKILL.md update, tests pass (GREEN).

## Structural Boundary Tests to Write
File: tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh

1. test_skill_references_sprint_pause_state_script
   Asserts sprint SKILL.md contains a reference to 'sprint-pause-state.sh' (the interface boundary — SKILL.md must name the script so the LLM has instructions to call it)
   Verify: grep -q "sprint-pause-state.sh" <SKILL.md path>

2. test_skill_references_resume_for_pause
   Asserts sprint SKILL.md contains a reference to '--resume' in the context of pause state (the LLM needs to know to offer --resume on re-invocation)
   Verify: grep -q "\-\-resume" <SKILL.md path>  (this already exists in sprint SKILL.md for other purposes; the new section must add context for pause state specifically)

3. test_skill_pause_state_section_exists
   Asserts sprint SKILL.md has a section or subsection heading containing "pause" or "manual-pause" or "pause state"
   Verify: grep -qi "pause state\|manual.pause\|pause.state" <SKILL.md path>

Per behavioral-testing-standard.md Rule 5: these are structural boundary tests only — they assert the interface (reference to script) is present, not the prose content of the instructions.

## SKILL.md Update
After the structural tests are written and failing, add a pause-resume subsection to the sprint SKILL.md manual-pause handshake section (Story F = 9e66-3e5d scope) that instructs the LLM to:

1. At handshake entry: call `sprint-pause-state.sh init <epic-id>` to create/refresh the pause state file
2. When updating per-story answers: call `sprint-pause-state.sh write <epic-id> story_answers <json>` after each story is answered
3. On SIGURG/Ctrl-C: the registered SIGURG trap calls `_spause_sigurg_handler`; the state file is preserved for --resume
4. At sprint start (before autonomous drain): call `sprint-pause-state.sh stale-cleanup` to remove old state files; if `sprint-pause-state.sh is-fresh <epic-id>` exits 0, present the user with: "Found existing pause state for this epic. Re-invoke sprint with --resume (or the sprint resume context) to continue the handshake."
5. On --resume (when pause state exists): call `sprint-pause-state.sh resume-context <epic-id>` to get the first unanswered story; rehydrate the handshake prompt from that story forward
6. When flag planning.external_dependency_block_enabled=false: skip all pause state calls (sprint-pause-state.sh is a no-op, so these can be called unconditionally — but per flag semantics, the handshake itself is never reached)

This instruction set lives in the sprint SKILL.md section that covers the manual-pause handshake (to be added by Story F = 9e66-3e5d). This task adds the persistence sub-section within that context. If Story F has not yet added the manual-pause section, add a stub section with a note: "Pause state persistence (Story I = 889c-b223) — activated when the manual-pause handshake lands from Story F."

## Implementation Notes
- SKILL.md is a non-executable LLM instruction file; tests are structural boundary only (Rule 5)
- The structural tests assert the interface contract (script reference), not the prose
- Test file must be in tests/skills/ to follow the sprint test pattern (test-sprint-phase8-end-session-gate.sh et al.)
- chmod +x the test file immediately after creating it

## Acceptance Criteria
Universal:
- [ ] bash tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh passes all 3 tests (FAILED: 0)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh 2>&1 | grep "FAILED: 0"

Task-specific:
- [ ] tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 3 test functions
  Verify: grep -c "^test_skill_" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/skills/test-sprint-pause-state-skill-boundary.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 3)}'
- [ ] sprint SKILL.md contains reference to sprint-pause-state.sh
  Verify: grep -q "sprint-pause-state.sh" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md
- [ ] sprint SKILL.md references pause state in context of --resume
  Verify: grep -qi "pause state" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/skills/sprint/SKILL.md
- [ ] check-referential-integrity.sh passes (no dead path references)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/plugins/dso/hooks/check-referential-integrity.sh 2>&1 | grep -v "FAIL" | head -1
